• AI importance is logged as 'Neutral' and the agentic-platform discussion, Gen AI commentary and AI-RFP rating fields are all blank. The Merlin product line of Agentic AI, Merlin and Merlin Intake is Zycus-side tagging rather than a reflection of documented buyer asks.
• On the 89-minute March 30 commercial meeting with Whitney Parks, Kory Cooper and David Elford, the buyer's action items were strictly pricing-oriented: pricing templates, P2P invoice counts, contract volumes, T&E partner details, and module-based pricing options across P2P, P2P+Sourcing and P2P+Spin Analytics. AI, autonomous negotiation and Merlin did not surface once.
• All four captured meetings between March 12 and April 6 focus on P2P scoping, pricing and support model questions. With an explicit 'Neutral' AI posture and no buyer-voiced AI signal across nearly three hours of recorded discussion, this opportunity sits firmly in Resistant territory.

metrics.narrative: The pain is concrete and quantified on the business side, though hard ROI for Zycus is not yet modeled. On the 30 Mar demo the buyer described downstream P2P touching ~2,000 users vs ~10 upstream, and Zycus showed AI spend analysis surfacing a $1.3B consolidation opportunity among top 5 suppliers. On the 6 Apr proposal call David Elford confirmed the licensing basis: ~$250M annual P2P spend, $1.04B total spend, 1,500 procurement/intake users, 1,000 contracts, 225,000 invoices. The gap is that no Zycus-specific savings/ROI case has been built against those numbers; quantifying the efficiency gain (status-check time, ~10 staff doing follow-ups) into a dollar value is the move — and it arms David for the CFO conversation.

sources: Proposal call 6 Apr 2026 (David Elford full-suite intent)

champion.narrative: David Elford is the developing champion: he is driving the process, declared full-suite intent ('foolish of us not to consider going all the way,' 6 Apr), and controls internal access. Whitney Parks is an engaged supporting advocate handling requirements and data validation. What has developed is clear product preference and live commercial engagement; what is still missing is proven advocacy upward — David has gatekept executive access ('the process should move under his direction') and has not yet introduced the CFO/CPO or the AP team. The play is to convert David from gatekeeper to sponsor by handing him the references and the change-management plan as the artifacts he carries upward.

sources: Proposal call 6 Apr 2026 (David Elford: Coupa can serve us, change management is the issue)
Intro call 12 Mar 2026 (Zip mentioned, never onboarded)
competition.narrative: This is fundamentally a Coupa-incumbent displacement plus a 'do nothing / renew Coupa' risk, and the incumbent is actively defending. Coupa is the 6-year incumbent and remains the default fallback: David said on 6 Apr 'I have no doubt Coupa can serve our business... the issue is change management.' That makes inertia the real competitor. The 29 Jun Next Step flags a fresh threat: 'Coupa is going on-site Wednesday' — the incumbent will reinforce the relationship and reframe the decision around adoption risk before Zycus has delivered its references. Zip was mentioned once as a previously-looked-at intake tool but never onboarded (Corey Cooper, 12 Mar) and is dormant. Zycus wins by de-risking change management with Coupa-replacement references and a contractual adoption plan delivered BEFORE the on-site, and by leaning on its single-code-base agentic/Merlin Teams-native differentiation versus Coupa's acquisition-stitched stack.

identify_pain.narrative: Pain is clearly articulated and owned. The buyer is a 6-year Coupa customer evaluating alternatives because Coupa's spend-analysis module degraded after a ~2021 update (Corey Cooper, 12 Mar: 'it used to be a lot better... it's honestly been a pain point'), support/ticket response times slowed since the Thoma Bravo PE acquisition, search and catalog prioritization is poor pushing users to non-preferred vendors, and CLM/P2P systems are disconnected with manual supplier onboarding across multiple ERP instances (Whitney Parks, 19 Mar). The overriding pain owner is David Elford, who named change management for 2,000 users as 'the hard part' (6 Apr). Pain is real but not acute — the Coupa renewal is not until ~Q1 2027, so the renewal clock is the urgency lever to keep alive.

paper_process.narrative: Contracting mechanics are not yet visible. Commercials are settled in principle (5-year full S2P, software plus professional services amortized over implementation, per the 6 Apr call), but there is no evidence of legal review, MSA/SOW drafting, InfoSec/security review, or procurement channel. The Coupa renewal (~Jan/Feb 2027) and the stated ~6-month implementation act as the forcing function: signature must land by roughly early July to support a January go-live, which is now at risk. Naming a paper owner and pre-staging the SOW/MSA + InfoSec sequence would create the visibility currently missing.

sources: Proposal call 6 Apr 2026 (David Elford: internal sign-off required)
SF Next_Step__c 22 Jun and 29 Jun 2026 (planned CFO/CPO escalation)
economic_buyer.narrative: The economic buyer is not engaged, and access is champion-gated. David Elford (Director, Non-Merchandise Procurement) is driving the evaluation but is explicitly not the final approver: on 6 Apr he said the proposal must be socialized with 'a bunch of other internal folks that would need to sign off before we moved at all,' and he has insisted the process move under his direction. Budget authority sits above him with the CFO (who mandated the evaluation) and the CPO/his boss, but neither has been met. Because David has gated executive access, the path to power runs THROUGH him — the move is to arm David to broker a CFO/CPO alignment session, not to cold-outreach the EB and burn his goodwill.

decision_process.narrative: The process is understood at the front but undefined at the back. David runs the evaluation under his direction and said on 6 Apr he needs 'a couple weeks to socialize' the proposal internally before a deep-dive on iSource/iContract/iSupplier, with sign-off required from additional stakeholders including the AP team and Corey's eventual replacement. The 27 May call established the clock: a decision 'within the next month or two' to meet the ~Jan/Feb 2027 Coupa renewal given a ~6-month implementation. The 29 Jun commercial call moved pricing forward, but no formal back-half steps (final approval, legal/InfoSec, SOW/MSA) are scheduled. Mapping the approval chain and co-authoring a dated mutual close plan is the gap.

decision_criteria.narrative: Criteria are well documented from the discovery, technical-discovery and demo sequence. The buyer wants an end-to-end P2P replacement for Coupa, prioritized as P2P first, then expenses (T&E), then spend analytics (Corey Cooper, 12 Mar intro call). On 19 Mar Whitney Parks stressed customization flexibility: custom metadata fields, configurable approval chains, guided buying with preferred punch-out catalogs ranked first, plus unified taxonomy across P2P/expense/capex and broad ERP/CMMS integration. Microsoft Teams-native guided buying is a stated requirement. On 30 Mar configuration control over search/catalog ordering was called essential, and the buyer flagged Egencia (later Emburse) as the preferred T&E partner. The decisive criterion per David (6 Apr) is change management for 2,000 users.

Move: when David re-engages, re-anchor to January 2027 deadline as the immovable forcing function — 'if we don't lock contract signature by first week of July, we can't deliver January go-live, and you'll be forced to extend Coupa for another year, which locks in the support lag and analytics complexity your CFO asked you to solve. Let's finalize this week: deliver references, run AP workshop, secure CFO/CPO alignment, and move to contracting so we protect January cutover.' Tie every next step to the January deadline to create urgency buyer has lost.

value.name: Coupa
value.quote: I have no doubt Coupa can serve our business or you can serve our business. The issue is change management.
value.source: Proposal call 6 Apr 2026 (David Elford)
value.how_we_win: De-risk change management with Coupa-replacement references and a contractual adoption plan; lean on single-code-base agentic Merlin + Teams-native guided buying vs Coupa's acquisition-stitched, degraded analytics and slow PE-era support

value.name: Coupa
value.quote: RISK — Coupa is going onsite Wednesday (2 July 2026). Coupa's spend-analysis module was great 2020–2023, then they sunset it and the new module is cumbersome, 300 menus, users struggle. Support responsiveness has been a pain point; ticket delays since Thoma Bravo PE acquisition. I have no doubt Coupa can serve our business or you (Zycus) can serve our business; the issue is change management — 2,000 people used to doing this a certain way.
value.source: Next_Step__c 29 June 2026; Avoma Touchpoint 1 (12 March 2026) Whitney Parks; Avoma Touchpoint 4 (6 April 2026) David Elford
value.how_we_win: Unified platform consolidating Bass Pro's three separate Coupa instances into single Zycus instance with one chart of accounts; Microsoft Teams-native Merlin Intake eliminating end-user training burden and increasing 2,000-user adoption vs Coupa's standalone UI; AI spend analytics with single taxonomy across P2P/T&E/capex addressing Coupa's post-2021 300-menu UI complexity; contractual change-management plan (workshops, lunch-and-learns, on-site support) de-risking the adoption challenge David stated is 'the hard part'; customer references from prior Coupa P2P replacements proving 2,000+ user adoption success (requested 27 May, must deliver before 2 July Coupa onsite); executive alignment session with CFO and the deal owner's manager this week to position strategic value case (CFO-mandated best-of-breed validation, long-term platform stability vs Coupa PE product churn) and block Coupa's renewal inertia play before their 2 July onsite re-anchors the relationship.

I have no doubt Coupa can serve our business or you can serve our business. The issue is change management, and we've got 2,000 people who are used to doing this a certain way.
